evals.name: framework-tanstack-router-v1
evals.prompt: I'm spinning up a new React app and I want to use TanStack Router v1 (the current file-based routing one). Every time I let Claude touch routing it suggests react-router-dom code or weird v0 TanStack stuff it half-remembers. Can you set something up so future sessions actually follow v1 patterns? I care most about route definitions, loaders, and search params.
evals.expected_output: A reference pack under docs/references/tanstack-router-v1/ (INDEX.md + topic files covering what's new in v1, route definitions, loaders/data loading, search params, migration from v0/react-router, and anti-patterns), a populated metadata.yaml, raw/ folder with downloaded originals, and an appended block in the project's CLAUDE.md under '## Framework/API References' with a specific when-to-consult phrase mentioning routing/loaders/search-params.

evals.name: api-deepgram-tts-v3
evals.prompt: We're integrating Deepgram's TTS API into our voice agent. The current one is the v3 generation with the Aura voices. Before I let Claude write the integration I want to pin it to the actual current docs so it doesn't make up request field names — last time it confidently used voice_id when the real field is model, wasted an hour. Get something in place please.
evals.expected_output: A reference pack under docs/references/deepgram-tts-v3/ (INDEX.md + topic files covering request/response shape with exact field names, auth, a quick-start with working curl+SDK examples, streaming/WebSocket if applicable, error handling), a populated metadata.yaml, no raw/ folder (api mode is synthesis), and an appended block in CLAUDE.md under '## Framework/API References'. Every wire-format claim must cite a Deepgram docs URL. Must include a 'Common mistakes' block calling out the voice_id vs model confusion.

evals.name: idioms-modern-typescript
evals.prompt: My biggest pain with Claude on TypeScript projects is that it defaults to like 2020-era code — enums everywhere, `any` casts, namespace, class-heavy. I want a reference that anchors it to modern idiomatic TypeScript: satisfies, discriminated unions, branded types, biome over eslint+prettier, that kind of thing. Build me something so every TS session leans on it.
evals.expected_output: A reference pack under docs/references/modern-idiomatic-typescript/ (INDEX.md + topic files covering prefer-X-over-Y substitutions, version-specific features like satisfies and const type parameters, anti-patterns like enum/namespace/any, project scaffolding with biome and tsconfig guidance, and API/signature style), a populated metadata.yaml, no raw/ folder, and an appended block in CLAUDE.md under '## Framework/API References' with consult-when phrased around 'writing or refactoring TypeScript code.' Every prescription must cite an authoritative source URL (Matt Pocock, matklad, TkDodo, Effective TypeScript, biome docs, etc.). Must triangulate — no prescription rests on a single blog post only.
